LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-08-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF VADIM KOGAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS UWE GUEHNEMANN, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON AUGUST 23, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL REAL PROPERTY CASES PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. BOTH PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR RESPECTIVE FILINGS IN SUPPORT OF AND IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 501 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE AND DEPARTMENT NO LATER THAN AUGUST 13, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-23-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS UWE GUEHNEMANN IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). ADDITIONALLY, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHEN THE CONTRACTOR COMPLETED OR CEASED PERFORMANCE OF THE ACT OR CONTRACT AT ISSUE. THE UNTIMELY SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION FILED BY THE DEFENDANT IS STRICKEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Upon Stipulation Releasing $10,000 Writ Of Attachment Cash Bond With Court Clerk And $21,864.94 Held By San Francisco Sheriff Pursuant To Issued Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Feb-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VADIM KOGAN'S Motion For Order Upon Stipulation Releasing $10,000 Writ Of Attachment Cash Bond With Court Clerk And $21,864.94 Held By San Francisco Sheriff Pursuant To Issued Writ Of Attachment

The parties have settled this matter and have stipulated to two orders. With this motion, they seek entry of the orders. No opposition was filed.

Pursuant to the terms of the parties' settlement, and for good cause shown in the moving papers, IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the San Francisco County Sheriff shall disburse the amount of funds held under writ of attachment at the San Francisco County Sheriff under its Sheriff's File No. 23476111, representing the principal sum of $21,864.94 attached at JP Morgan Chase Bank with account number ending in xxx5225, account of DEFCO, UWE GUEHNEMANN, plus any interest accrued thereon, less any Sheriff's costs, to Plaintiff Vadim Kogan through his attorney of record. Said funds shall be mailed by the Sheriff to Plaintiff's Attorney, Mark J. Rice, Esq., McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, 55 Professional Center Parkway, Suite A, San Rafael, CA 94903, as part of the settlement of the above case. Upon such payment the writ of attachment shall be discharged.

IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that this court's treasurer and/or disbursing officer shall issue its warrant or check made payable to Plaintiff Vadim Kogan in the cash sum of $10,000 in principal, plus any applicable earned interest thereon, as and for the return to Plaintiff of the Deposit of Undertaking, Transaction Number H2423C21D008, Tag #037485, deposited with the court by Plaintiff Kogan on December 21, 2023. The court shall mail said funds to Plaintiff's attorney, Mark J. Rice, McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, 55 Professional Center Parkway, Suite A, San Rafael, CA 94903.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The Second Cause Of Action Against Defendants Uwe Guehnemann
Off Calendar Aug-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Mark Rice; 415-472-3434; Mcneil, Silverira, Rice & Wiley; counsel appearing for plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Mr. Rice contests the courts tentative ruling and informs the court proper notice was given to all parties. Hearing held. Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VADIM KOGAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of The Second Cause Of Action Against Defendants Uwe Guehnemann is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Additionally, there is a triable issue of fact as to when the contractor completed or ceased performance of the act or contract at issue. The untimely supplemental declaration filed by the Defendant is stricken.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
